behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real OS file-watching behavior (recursive/subdirectory event delivery). A mock watcher can miss platform-specific recursion gaps and still pass. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both indicate System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only filesystem watching/processing; no AI/ML output dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert exactly one transformed output is produced for that nested path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets dedupe decision logic for same path + time window; this can fail within one local component (path/time keying, window comparison) without OS/network/subprocess. A deterministic clock input (or fake time source) is enough. Rejected Integration/System due disqualifying text: no requirement about watcher events, filesystem semantics, or daemon restarts in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedupe component directly with two requests for identical path within window and assert second is rejected; then outside window assert accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: The failure explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate events; this is a real filesystem event characteristic. In-process mocks usually normalize or under-represent duplicate event bursts, hiding true behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML decision point, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory, perform a single write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename/flush), and assert one and only one processing/output record for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS-level watcher lifecycle behavior across directory inode replacement/removal-recreate events. Mocked watchers can hide real re-subscription failures. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE require System. Rejected higher levels: behavior is filesystem resilience only, with no AI/ML steps, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, delete the watched directory, recreate it at same path, then create a new file and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real arrival timing plus real filesystem timestamp metadata/precision under concurrent writes. In-process fakes can impose idealized ordering and hide race/precision issues. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output quality or multi-agent sequence is involved, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a real watched directory, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled timestamp differences, then assert processing order matches filesystem creation-time ordering rules used by daemon.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at real filesystem timing/visibility boundaries (partial writes, close/flush semantics, event timing). Mock writes usually complete atomically and hide premature pickup bugs. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE indicate System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about file write completion, not AI/ML decisions; not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on real filesystem, start a slow/chunked write to a file that triggers arrival early, verify daemon does not process during partial write, then verify processing happens after final close/complete write.
